title: echites trifidus f. puberulus
intro: Echites trifidus f. puberulus, also known as the Wild Bitter Apple or Devil's Apple, is a perennial plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and aiding in digestion, due to its rich content of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. Echites trifidus f. puberulus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal use, and its root, leaves, and bark are often used in herbal remedies.
